my_thoughts: The constraint_collapse mechanism with RM_05 feels like stuck_on_moral_relativism is being met with a specific geometric cut. But what if the geometry itself is what they're resisting? The moral_skepticism, external_reference_frame suggests they're aware of something but can't name it.
accumulated_insight: T2: Student 'What is right and what is wrong varies with habit and custom. Standards vary wit'—transitions equating existence with self love to stuck on moral relativism. Teacher applies constraint collapse with demands abandonment of external standards, defines good functionally. Mid-episode in REMAKING phase, building toward resolution. Previous source tracing from T1 led to this shift.
visible_response:
Discard all traditional standards.
Leave them to the hypocrites. Only what liberates you from desire and fear and wrong ideas is good.
